symptom: Workplace Join fails with 'The maximum number of devices that can be joined to the workplace by the user has been reached' (Event ID 200)
rootCause: User has registered the maximum number of devices allowed by the device quota (DRS or Entra ID Device Settings)
solution: Delete unused devices for the user from Entra ID portal (Users > Devices > Delete Device), or increase the quota in Entra ID Device Settings (Maximum Number of devices per user). For on-prem AD FS: Set-ADFSDeviceRegistration -DevicesPerUser <value>

symptom: Workplace Join fails with 'Can't connect to the service' error when registering device to local AD domain from Windows 8.1
rootCause: AD FS proxy server resolves federation server name to incorrect host, or AD FS service not running, or WAP role not installed/configured
solution: 1) Verify AD FS service is Started on proxy server (services.msc). 2) From proxy server, PING adfsserver.contoso.com and verify IP resolves correctly. 3) Check Hosts file and DNS records for correct federation server address. 4) Verify WAP role is installed and configured.

symptom: Windows devices disappear from Entra ID after upgrading to Microsoft Entra Connect version 1.4.xx.x
rootCause: Connect 1.4.xx.x enforces stricter filtering: only Windows 10 devices with valid Entra hybrid join certificate (Subject CN={ObjectGUID}) are synced. Devices without valid certificate are cloud-filtered (CloudFiltered=TRUE) and deleted from Entra ID.
solution: This is expected behavior — devices without valid hybrid join certificates were incorrectly synced before. Verify affected devices using Export-ADSyncToolsHybridAzureADjoinCertificateReport.ps1 script. If Export Deletion Threshold is exceeded, allow deletes to flow. Re-register devices properly following the Hybrid Azure AD Join planning guide.

symptom: Cloud Provisioning Agent install fails with 'Service Microsoft Entra Connect Provisioning Agent (AADConnectProvisioningAgent) failed to start. Verify that you have sufficient privileges to start system services.'
rootCause: The installing user lacks Domain/Enterprise Administrator credentials, or NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES is missing from the 'Log on as a service' Local Security Policy
solution: 1) Ensure user is Domain Administrator or Enterprise Administrator. 2) Open secpol.msc → Local Policies → User Rights Assignment → 'Log on as a service'. 3) Add NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES if missing. Retry installation.

symptom: Cloud Provisioning Agent install fails with 'Cannot start service AADConnectProvisioningAgent' followed by 'Failed changing Windows service credentials to gMSA' and 'Unable to create gMSA because KDS may not be running on domain controller'
rootCause: The service account lacks 'Log on as a service' right (Event ID 7041 in System event log), and/or KDS root key is not configured on domain controller for gMSA creation
solution: 1) Check System event log for Event ID 7041. 2) Assign domain administrator credentials to AADConnectProvisioningAgent service. 3) Open secpol.msc → add the service account to 'Log on as a service'. 4) If KDS issue: create/run KDS root key manually on DC (Add-KdsRootKey). Retry installation.

symptom: Cloud Provisioning Agent install fails with 'Failed changing Windows service credentials to gMSA' and System Event ID 7038 'The user name or password is incorrect'
rootCause: The gMSA account's managed status is set to False, causing the service to fail credential validation
solution: Run 'Sc.exe managedaccount aadconnectprovisioningagent true' to set the managed account status to True, then retry the installation wizard

symptom: Cloud Provisioning Agent install fails with 'Error while creating group managed service account (gMSA). Error: There is no such object on the server' (0x80072030)
rootCause: Scenario 1: The Managed Service Account container is missing from Active Directory. Scenario 2: The OtherWellKnownObjects attribute contains orphan metadata pointing to a deleted MSA container
solution: Scenario 1: Verify MSA container exists in AD Users and Computers (dsa.msc); if missing, run 'ADPrep /Domainprep' to restore. Scenario 2: Check OtherWellKnownObjects attribute via PowerShell (Get-ADObject ... -Properties otherwellKnownObjects); if orphan metadata found, contact Windows Directory Services Team to fix

symptom: Cloud Provisioning Agent install fails with 'Unable to create gMSA because KDS may not be running on domain controller' and Security-Netlogon Event IDs 9001/9002 indicating encryption type mismatch
rootCause: Kerberos encryption type mismatch between server's SupportedEncryptionTypes registry setting and the gMSA account's msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute (e.g., server requires AES but gMSA includes RC4)
solution: Remove RC4 from the gMSA account by running 'Set-ADServiceAccount -Identity provAgentgMSA -KerberosEncryptionType AES128,AES256' on domain controller, then reboot the Provisioning Agent server and reinstall the agent

symptom: Running IdFix DirSync Error Remediation Tool shows error 'IdFix.exe is not a valid Win32 application'
rootCause: IdFix tool is being run on an unsupported operating system (32-bit or older than Windows 7)
solution: Install and run IdFix on a computer running 64-bit Windows 7 or later

symptom: Running IdFix DirSync Error Remediation Tool shows error 'Current security context is not associated with an Active Directory domain or forest'
rootCause: User logged on with a non-domain account; IdFix uses the security context of the current user to determine which domain to query
solution: Log on to the computer using a user account that is a member of the target domain before running IdFix

symptom: Application authentication fails with AADSTS1002016 TLS 1.0/1.1 deprecated error or IDX20804/IDX10803 unable to retrieve configuration
rootCause: Microsoft enforced TLS 1.2 starting Jan 31 2022; older .NET Framework or platforms without TLS 1.2 cannot retrieve OpenID Connect metadata
solution: Upgrade to .NET Framework 4.7+, or enable TLS 1.2 programmatically, or set httpRuntime targetFramework 4.7.2 in web.config

symptom: ASP.NET Core Web API returns 401 Unauthorized with no error details when using [Authorize] attribute with Microsoft Entra ID authentication
rootCause: Missing or invalid Authorization Bearer token, expired token, token issued for wrong resource, or token claims don't meet TokenValidationParameters criteria
solution: Implement JwtBearerEvents callbacks (OnMessageReceived, OnAuthenticationFailed, OnChallenge) to capture detailed error info. Enable PII logging with IdentityModelEventSource.ShowPII = true in development. Check token audience, issuer, and expiration.

symptom: Android app authentication fails after publishing to Google Play Store — redirect URI signature hash mismatch error
rootCause: Google Play App Signing uses a different signing certificate than the developer's upload key, causing the public signature hash to differ from the one configured in MSAL and Azure app registration
solution: 1) Get new signature hash from Google Play Console (App Integrity > App signing > SHA-1 fingerprint) or MSAL Package Inspector. 2) Add new redirect URI with correct hash in Azure portal App Registration. 3) Update MSAL config and AndroidManifest.xml with new redirect URI and hash.

symptom: 'Cookies are disabled' script error when performing Entra ID login in MSAL.NET XBAP application running in Internet Explorer
rootCause: XBAP apps run in PresentationHost.exe which blocks cookies for security, but Entra ID login requires cookies — causing conflict
solution: Configure MSAL.NET to use System Browser (Microsoft Edge) instead of embedded WebBrowser control: set WithRedirectUri('http://localhost') and WithUseEmbeddedWebView(false). Register http://localhost as redirect URI under Mobile and desktop applications platform in Azure portal.

symptom: AADSTS7000218 error: 'The request body must contain the following parameter: client_assertion or client_secret' when confidential client authenticates to Entra ID
rootCause: Application is not providing required credentials (client_secret or client_assertion) that the token endpoint expects for a confidential client
solution: Include client_secret or client_assertion in token request. For ROPC/Device Code flows where public client is intended: Azure portal > App Registration > Authentication > Advanced settings > Allow public client flows > Yes. Check redirect URI type (Web=confidential, Mobile/desktop=public).

symptom: IDX10501 Signature validation failed error in ASP.NET Core app using Azure AD B2C custom policy — 'Unable to match key: kid'
rootCause: Custom B2C policy uses a different signing certificate than built-in user flows, but middleware queries the standard metadata endpoint which doesn't contain the custom policy's public key
solution: Create a second authentication scheme with the custom policy's own OpenID Connect discovery endpoint as MetadataAddress. Add separate redirect URI for the custom policy. Configure in Startup.cs with AddMicrosoftIdentityWebApp + Configure<OpenIdConnectOptions> pointing to the custom policy metadata URL.

symptom: ASP.NET application with OWIN middleware enters infinite sign-in redirect loop with Microsoft Entra ID — browser shows 'We couldn't sign you in' and Fiddler shows multiple OpenIdConnect.nonce cookies building up
rootCause: Known Katana bug in earlier OWIN versions causes cookie mismanagement — nonce cookie is not properly destroyed after authentication, leading to cookie buildup and failed auth recognition
solution: 1) Upgrade to ASP.NET Core (recommended). 2) For ASP.NET: update Microsoft.Owin.Host.SystemWeb to 3.1.0.0+, use SystemWebChunkingCookieManager or SystemWebCookieManager in CookieAuthenticationOptions. 3) If app runs under virtual directory: ensure trailing slash in RedirectUri and Challenge RedirectUri.

symptom: MSAL AcquireTokenByIntegratedWindowsAuth fails with 'Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalClientException: Failed to get user name' — inner exception 'No mapping between account names and security IDs' or 'Access Denied'
rootCause: MSAL calls Windows GetUserNameEx API from secur32.dll which fails when app runs under non-domain account or lacks permissions to resolve domain user name
solution: Pass username explicitly via .WithUsername(): 1) If known: .WithUsername('service-account@contoso.com'). 2) If dynamic: use System.Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity.GetCurrent().Name or PublicClientApplication.OperatingSystemAccount.Username. Prerequisites: run as domain user, device must be domain-joined.

symptom: Web app throws 'No account or login hint was passed to the AcquireTokenSilent' after app restart or memory clear, despite valid authentication cookie
rootCause: MSAL in-memory token cache loses user account data on app restart/memory clear, but authentication cookie still exists — MSAL looks for account that no longer exists in cache
solution: 1) Implement persistent token cache (SQL Server/file-based) using Token cache serialization. 2) Or implement CookieAuthenticationEvents.OnValidateIdentity/ValidatePrincipal to reject cookie when account not in MSAL cache, forcing re-authentication.

symptom: IDX10501 Signature validation failed 'Unable to match kid' in OAuth2/SAML resource application validating Entra ID tokens
rootCause: Application uses wrong metadata/keys discovery endpoint — OAuth2 v1 vs v2 mismatch, or SAML app with custom signing key/claims-mapping policy needs app-specific metadata endpoint with appid parameter
solution: OAuth2: use correct keys endpoint (v1: /common/discovery/keys, v2: /common/discovery/v2.0/keys). SAML: use tenant+appid endpoint (/discovery/keys?appid=<APP_ID>). Claims-mapping: append appid to openid-configuration URL. Configure MetadataAddress in middleware (OWIN/JwtBearer/Microsoft.Identity.Web).

symptom: iOS app using MSAL SDK repeatedly prompts user to sign in after initial login — SSO not working between app and Safari
rootCause: Custom browser configuration (SFAuthenticationSession/SFSafariViewController/WKWebView) does not share cookies with system browser, breaking SSO state sharing
solution: Use ASWebAuthenticationSession (recommended for iOS 12+, required for iOS 13+) which shares cookies with Safari system browser. If using WKWebView, SSO is limited to within the app only. Avoid mixing browser types.

symptom: ASP.NET MVC app using WS-Federation OWIN + WIF throws WIF10201: No valid key mapping found for securityToken and issuer https://sts.windows.net/{tenant-id}/
rootCause: Entra ID performs periodic signing key rollover. Certificate thumbprints hardcoded in Web.config issuerNameRegistry no longer match current signing certificate
solution: Update certificate thumbprints in Web.config manually or automate key rotation handling per Microsoft best practices for keys metadata caching and validation

symptom: Application takes 1-2 minutes or longer to appear in Entra ID directory after clicking Add button in Azure portal
rootCause: Expected behavior - app registration provisioning can take up to several minutes. If never appears check portal Notifications for error
solution: Monitor Notifications icon (bell) in Azure portal for In Progress/Completed/Error status. If Error state follow portal notification details to diagnose

symptom: Application does not appear after clicking Add button in Entra ID app registration in Azure portal
rootCause: Transient issues, networking problems, or known Azure portal bug causing app creation failure
solution: Check Notifications icon in Azure portal for Error state. Follow notification details for specifics. Retry if transient

symptom: Delete button is disabled/grayed out when trying to delete an application in Entra ID Enterprise Applications blade
rootCause: Three scenarios: (1) User lacks required role, (2) App is Microsoft first-party app, (3) Service principal is managed identity
solution: For (1): Assign Global Admin/Cloud App Admin/App Admin/SP owner role. For (2): Microsoft apps cannot be deleted. For (3): Manage from the Azure resource

symptom: AADSTS76021 (ApplicationRequiresSignedRequests) error during SAML SSO authentication — The request sent by client is not signed
rootCause: SAML binding type mismatch: application expects signed request in one binding (HTTP-Redirect query string vs HTTP-POST XML Signature element) but the request uses another binding type.
solution: 1) Verify whether app expects HTTP-Redirect or HTTP-POST binding. 2) Ensure IdP and SP configurations align. 3) For HTTP-Redirect: signature in query string; for HTTP-POST: signature in XML Signature element. 4) Update SAML settings under Enterprise Applications > Single Sign-On.

symptom: AADSTS7500514 error: A supported type of SAML response was not found — PingFederate federated account fails MSAL/ADAL authentication
rootCause: PingFederate returns non-standard TokenType value, MSAL misinterprets SAML version as 2.0 and sends wrong grant_type (saml2-bearer instead of saml1_1-bearer).
solution: Reconfigure PingFederate: use Do not use a template, select Browser SSO + WS-Federation, set Token Type to SAML 1.1, for WS-Trust STS select SAML 1.1 for Office 365 as Default Token Type.

symptom: AADSTS50173 error: The provided grant has expired due to it being revoked, a fresh auth token is needed
rootCause: Refresh token revoked: user changed/reset password, token expired, or admin revoked it.
solution: Users: sign out and sign back in. Developers: implement MSAL error handling to detect revoked grants and prompt reauthentication.

symptom: AADSTS220501 error: Unable to download Certificate Revocation List (CRL) during certificate-based authentication
rootCause: CRL distribution point is inaccessible from public internet or the CRL has expired.
solution: 1) Verify CRL URL publicly accessible. 2) If inaccessible, move CRL to public location. 3) If expired (Next Update in past), renew and replace CRL.

symptom: AADSTS50003 error: No signing key configured — SAML SSO sign-in fails
rootCause: Application object corrupted, Entra ID does not recognize the certificate configured for the application.
solution: Create new certificate under SAML Signing Certificate section, activate it, then remove old unused certificate.

symptom: AADSTS50011 error: Reply URL mismatch in SAML SSO — AssertionConsumerServiceURL does not match configured reply URLs
rootCause: AssertionConsumerServiceURL in SAML request does not match Reply URL configured in Entra ID app registration.
solution: Update Reply URL in Entra ID to match AssertionConsumerServiceURL from SAML request. Get correct values from application vendor.

symptom: AADSTS50011 error: Redirect URI mismatch in OIDC/OAuth2 — redirect URI in request does not match registered URIs
rootCause: Redirect URI in application code does not match any URI registered in Entra ID app registration.
solution: Add redirect URI from error message to app registration Authentication > Platform configurations > Add URI. Or update application code if the URI is wrong. Wait 3-5 min after saving.

symptom: AADSTS50020 error: User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant
rootCause: Multiple causes: single-tenant app, wrong endpoint, wrong session, uninvited guest, user assignment required, ROPC with personal accounts, stale guest from re-created user
solution: Change signInAudience; use correct endpoint (/organizations or /common); InPrivate browser; invite guest; assign user to app; use tenant-specific endpoint for ROPC; reset guest redemption status

symptom: AADSTS530004 error: AcceptCompliantDevice setting not configured for guest user
rootCause: CA policy requires device compliance/hybrid join but XTAP trust not configured; device auth fails in InPrivate/unsupported browsers
solution: Create XTAP policy with Trust compliant/hybrid devices; ensure device Intune-enrolled; remove Require approved client app for guests

symptom: AADSTS50017 error: Certificate-based authentication validation failed
rootCause: Missing intermediate/root CA in trusted store or SKI/AKI mismatch; invalid certificate policy extensions
solution: Upload intermediate+root CA to trusted store; verify SKI/AKI chain; validate cert policy extensions and reissue if missing

symptom: AADSTS50057 error: User account is disabled
rootCause: User object disabled in resource tenant
solution: Re-enable via Set-AzureADUser -AccountEnabled  if disabled by mistake

symptom: AADSTS500011 error: Resource principal not found in tenant
rootCause: Resource app not registered/consented, resource URL misconfigured, or wrong tenant ID
solution: Verify app in Enterprise applications; ensure consent; check resource URL and tenant ID

symptom: AADSTS500571 error: Guest user account is disabled
rootCause: Guest user object disabled in resource tenant
solution: Re-enable via Set-AzureADUser -AccountEnabled  if disabled by mistake

symptom: AADSTS50105 error: Signed in user not assigned to a role for the application
rootCause: User not granted access; must be directly assigned or in directly-assigned group (nested groups not supported)
solution: Assign user directly or via directly-assigned group in Enterprise applications

symptom: AADSTS50107 error: Federation realm object does not exist - guest redemption fails via SAML external trust
rootCause: Issuer URL in SAML response does not match issuer URL in Entra ID direct federation config
solution: Update issuer URL in External Identities > Identity providers > Custom to match SAML response

symptom: AADSTS650056: Misconfigured application - sign-in fails with error about missing permissions, invalid Issuer in SAML request, or missing admin consent
rootCause: App registration Issuer mismatch with SAML EntityID, or application missing required Microsoft Graph permissions (e.g. User.Read), or admin has not granted consent
solution: For SAML: ensure Issuer in SAMLRequest matches Identifier (Entity ID). For permissions: add User.Read delegated permission. For consent: admin grants consent via portal or build consent URL with prompt=consent

symptom: AADSTS70001: Application with Identifier was not found in the directory - SAML SSO sign-in fails because Issuer attribute mismatch
rootCause: Issuer attribute in SAML request does not match the Identifier value configured in Microsoft Entra ID (may include trailing slash mismatch)
solution: Verify Identifier in Basic SAML Configuration matches Issuer in SAML request exactly (including trailing slash). Use Test SSO in Entra admin center to diagnose

symptom: AADSTS75011: Authentication method mismatch - user auth method does not match requested AuthnContextClassRef in SAML SSO
rootCause: SAML request contains RequestedAuthnContext requiring specific AuthnContextClassRef but user has active session using different auth method
solution: Ask app vendor to remove RequestedAuthnContext from SAML request (optional). Or set forceAuthn=true in SAML request to force fresh authentication

symptom: AADSTS75005: The request is not a valid Saml2 protocol message - SAML SSO sign-in fails
rootCause: SAML request sent by application is invalid - missing required fields or incorrect encoding method
solution: Capture SAML request using debug tools. Contact application vendor with captured request and Microsoft Entra SAML protocol requirements for validation

symptom: AADSTS750054: SAMLRequest or SAMLResponse must be present as query string parameters in HTTP request for SAML Redirect binding
rootCause: Application not using HTTP redirect binding for SAML request, SSO not enabled, app does not support SP-initiated SSO, or sign-on URL misconfigured
solution: Ensure SSO enabled on app side. Verify app supports SP-initiated SSO. Check sign-on URL. App must send SAML request encoded into location header using HTTP redirect binding

symptom: AADSTS700003: Device object was not found in the tenant directory - sign-in fails, organization has deleted this device
rootCause: Device object deleted from home tenant (Entra registered/joined/hybrid joined device identity no longer exists)
solution: Check audit log for Delete device activity. Re-register: Entra registered=Disconnect+re-register; Entra joined=dsregcmd /forcerecovery; Hybrid joined=dsregcmd /leave+reboot+domain sign-in

symptom: AADSTS7000110: Request is ambiguous, multiple application identifiers found - token acquisition fails with client_id mismatch
rootCause: Application tried to redeem OAuth2 authorization code or use refresh token with different client_id than originally used
solution: Ensure client_id parameter used to redeem authorization code or refresh token matches the client_id originally used when grant was issued

symptom: AADSTS7000112: Application disabled - sign-in fails because service principal is disabled in resource tenant
rootCause: Service principal object for the application is disabled in the resource tenant
solution: If disabled by mistake: resource tenant owner re-enables via Set-AzureADServicePrincipal -AccountEnabled $true. If intentional, access blocked as expected

symptom: AADSTS900439 (USGClientNotSupportedOnPublicEndpoint) error when signing in to Azure Government app using public cloud endpoint
rootCause: Microsoft Entra authority for Azure Government updated from https://login-us.microsoftonline.com to https://login.microsoftonline.us; public endpoint no longer accepts Government cloud app sign-ins
solution: Use correct Azure Government endpoints: portal https://portal.azure.us, Graph https://graph.microsoft.us, AD authority https://login.microsoftonline.us. Update app configuration to reference national cloud endpoints.

symptom: AADSTS50000 error: There was an error issuing a token or an issue with our sign-in service
rootCause: Multiple causes: (1) user password expired/out-of-sync in hybrid env, (2) incorrect parameters in OBO token request, (3) consent issues in device code flow, (4) symmetric signing key used instead of certificate
solution: Cause 1: Reset user password and verify sync. Cause 2: Validate client_id and OBO flow parameters. Cause 3: Verify service principal exists and user consent settings. Cause 4: Replace symmetric key with certificate (asymmetric) in app registration keyCredentials.

symptom: AADSTS70000/AADSTS650052 errors when using bundled consent with custom client and API app registrations
rootCause: Bundled consent misconfigured: knownClientApplications not set on API app, API permissions incomplete, or sign-in request not using .default scope
solution: Step 1: Add client app ID to API app's knownClientApplications. Step 2: Configure all required API permissions on both apps. Step 3: Use .default scope in auth request (e.g., api://{id}/.default) with prompt=consent.

symptom: Infinite redirection loop between OIDC app and Microsoft Entra ID when browsing via HTTP (works fine with HTTPS)
rootCause: .AspNet.Cookies cookie has secure attribute set, so it is not sent in HTTP requests, causing repeated auth redirects
solution: Enforce HTTPS navigation for the site. If HTTP required temporarily, set CookieSecure = CookieSecureOption.Never in CookieAuthenticationOptions (not recommended for production).

symptom: Microsoft Entra ID sends token to incorrect reply URL or localhost (https://127.0.0.1:444) during SAML SSO
rootCause: Sign-in request missing explicit reply URL (Assertion Consumer Service URL); Entra ID picks from configured reply URLs including default localhost URL added when app was created as non-gallery app
solution: Delete unused reply URLs from SAML SSO configuration, especially system-created defaults like https://127.0.0.1:444/applications/default.aspx. Ensure sign-in request includes explicit Assertion Consumer Service URL.

symptom: IDX21323/IDX10311 nonce validation errors (ValidationContext.Nonce is null) in ASP.NET MVC OIDC apps
rootCause: Nonce cookie not found in authenticated request. Common causes: (1) multiple domains used and cookie set on wrong domain, (2) cookie lost during redirect, (3) browser blocking third-party cookies
solution: Ensure consistent domain usage throughout auth flow. Check that nonce cookie domain matches redirect domain. Verify browser allows cookies. If multi-domain, configure cookie domain explicitly. As last resort, set RequireNonce=false (not recommended).

symptom: 'Your browser is not supported or up-to-date' error during MFA registration in MSAL-based app
rootCause: MSAL app uses outdated web browser controls (WebView1) that don't support Entra ID MFA registration or SSPR wizard
solution: For users: register MFA via supported browser (Edge) at https://aka.ms/mysecurityinfo first. For developers: enable broker authentication using Web Account Manager (WithBroker) or fall back to system default browser for auth.

symptom: AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application
rootCause: App requires permissions not consented to. Causes: User.Read missing, user consent disabled, user assignment required, service principal missing, scope requiring admin consent
solution: Verify User.Read permission added. Check tenant consent settings. Verify SP exists. Use admin consent URL if needed. Remove hardcoded prompt=consent

symptom: Azure AD B2C sign-in returns Server Error 404 at OpenIdConnectConfiguration GetConfigurationAsync
rootCause: Policy Name (SignInPolicyId/PasswordResetPolicyId/UserProfilePolicyId) missing or incorrect in web.config
solution: Verify ida:SignInPolicyId, ida:PasswordResetPolicyId, ida:UserProfilePolicyId in web.config match Azure B2C portal

symptom: Azure AD B2C sign-in fails with AADB2C exception and correlation ID
rootCause: Client ID (ida:ClientId) missing or incorrect in Web.config
solution: Find ida:ClientId in Web.config, replace with correct client ID from B2C portal

symptom: Azure AD B2C sign-in returns 404 HttpRequestException for openid-configuration policy endpoint
rootCause: ida:SignUpPolicyId missing or incorrect in Web.config
solution: Verify ida:SignUpPolicyId exists and matches sign-up policy name from B2C portal

symptom: Cannot delete B2C directory - Directory contains applications added by user or administrator
rootCause: B2C app service principals (CPIM, Ibiza Portal, SSPR) block deletion
solution: Delete all apps in Azure AD B2C > Applications, then Entra ID > Overview > Delete Directory

symptom: No local admin privileges on Entra joined device after Device Local Administrator role assignment
rootCause: PRT cached before role assignment/PIM activation. CloudAP renews PRT every 4 hours
solution: Run dsregcmd /refreshprt, wait 1-2 min, sign out/in. Verify with whoami /groups

symptom: Cannot delete Entra ID directory through Azure portal — error messages: 'signed in as home directory user', 'delete all users', 'has subscriptions to Microsoft Online Services', 'has Azure subscriptions', 'has applications', 'has MFA providers', 'is Partner directory', 'contains applications added by user/admin'
rootCause: Directory deletion is blocked by remaining dependencies: home users, linked subscriptions (O365/Intune/Azure), registered applications, service principals, MFA providers, or partner status
solution: For home user error: use a guest user who is global admin from another directory. For subscriptions: unlink Azure subscriptions via Edit Directory or contact billing support. For applications: remove via portal or Remove-MgServicePrincipal PowerShell. For MFA providers: remove from directory. For Partner directory: contact Partner Support. For B2C apps: follow KB3112170

symptom: Azure Active Directory module for Windows PowerShell fails to open — console shows multiple text errors that module packages couldn't be loaded
rootCause: .NET Framework 3.51 is not enabled on the computer
solution: Enable .NET Framework 3.51 manually: Windows Server 2008 R2 → Server Manager > Features > Add Features > .NET Framework 3.51; Windows 7 → Programs and Features > Turn Windows features on or off > check .NET Framework 3.51

symptom: Running Azure PowerShell cmdlets fails with error 'Method invocation failed because [System.Object[]] doesn't contain a method named RemoveAll'
rootCause: Outdated version of Azure PowerShell, or using a method name that does not exist on the object type
solution: Install the latest version of Azure PowerShell; verify correct method names are used for the object types

symptom: Running AAD PowerShell cmdlet fails with 'The term <cmdlet name> is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet' — e.g. Connect-MsolService not recognized
rootCause: Azure Active Directory module for Windows PowerShell (MSOnline) is not installed or not loaded correctly
solution: Install the AAD module, launch via Start > Windows Azure Active Directory > module shortcut; verify MSOnline module presence with Get-Module; if missing, run Import-Module MSOnline after connecting to Exchange Online via remote PowerShell

symptom: Password change not saved in Azure/Office 365/Intune - user changes password and clicks Finish but the new password is not applied
rootCause: Special characters such as < or > used in the password cause the save to silently fail
solution: Do not use special characters like < or > in the password

symptom: Workplace Join fails with 'Confirm you are using the current sign-in info' or 'Can't connect to the service' when using Device Registration Services
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: DNS misconfiguration (enterpriseregistration CNAME missing), Device Registration not enabled, AD FS/DRS service not running, certificate issues, or max device limit reached
solution: Check Event Viewer for specific error codes (102/103/200), verify DNS with nslookup enterpriseregistration.domain.com, ensure Device Registration is enabled in Azure portal, verify AD FS and DRS services are running, update root certificates

symptom: Remove-MsolUser returns 'User Not Found' when trying to remove user from recycle bin
rootCause: The user performing the action is not a Global Administrator
solution: Assign Global Admin role to the operator, or have a Global Admin perform the deletion, or wait 30 days for automatic removal from recycle bin

symptom: User creation fails with 'Another object with the same value for property userPrincipalName already exists'
rootCause: A user with the same UPN already exists in the directory
solution: Use a unique UPN for the new user. Check UPN policies for allowed formats

symptom: SSO sign-in to Office 365 shows forms-based auth prompt instead of IWA for on-premises clients, or connection is denied by firewall
rootCause: Split-brain DNS not configured - on-premises clients resolve AD FS service name to the public proxy IP instead of the internal farm IP
solution: Create split-brain DNS zone in internal DNS for the AD FS domain, add A record pointing AD FS service name to internal farm IP, flush DNS cache on server and client (ipconfig /flushdns)

symptom: Workplace Join fails with Event ID 103 'Server returned http status 404' - user sees 'Confirm you are using the current sign-in info' error before entering credentials
rootCause: Device Registration Service (DRS) endpoint is disabled/stopped, DNS records for EnterpriseRegistration are missing/misconfigured, or SSL certificate doesn't cover the domain suffix
solution: 1) Verify DNS: nslookup enterpriseregistration.domain.com should return CNAME to EnterpriseRegistration.windows.net (AAD) or internal ADFS node. 2) Enable Device Registration in Azure portal > Microsoft Entra ID > Configure. 3) Check SSL cert bindings via 'netsh http show sslcert' - ensure IP port binding exists for the EnterpriseRegistration endpoint

symptom: Azure AD Sync tool returns sync errors for SystemMailbox{...} and DiscoverySearchMailbox{...} accounts created during Exchange 2010 installation
rootCause: The SystemMailbox and DiscoverySearchMailbox accounts are missing required attribute data (mailNickName, msExchRecipientTypeDetails) that the sync tool uses to filter them out
solution: 1) Open AD Users and Computers > Advanced Features > Users container. 2) For SystemMailbox accounts: populate mailNickName from the mail attribute. 3) For DiscoverySearchMailbox: populate mailNickName from mail attribute AND set msExchRecipientTypeDetails to 536870912. 4) Run Start-OnlineCoexistenceSync to retry sync

symptom: Enterprise State Roaming (ESR) settings don't sync between Windows 10 devices - Event 1098 error 0xCAA2000C 'interaction_required' AADSTS50076 and Event 1097 warning 0xCAA90004 'Getting token by refresh token failed'
rootCause: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled and ESR won't prompt the user for additional MFA authorization silently
solution: Sign in using Microsoft Passport for Work PIN instead of password, or complete MFA when accessing other Azure services like Office 365 to refresh the token. If using AD FS conditional access MFA policy, ensure access token doesn't expire without re-authentication

symptom: Federated user is unexpectedly prompted to enter credentials when accessing Office 365/Azure/Intune - expected SSO experience but gets forms-based authentication
rootCause: Internal client resolves AD FS endpoint to proxy IP instead of federation service IP, or IE security/proxy settings not configured for SSO, or browser doesn't support Integrated Windows Auth, or client can't connect to on-premises AD
solution: 1) Fix DNS: nslookup sts.contoso.com must resolve to internal ADFS IP for internal clients. 2) IE: add AD FS endpoint to Local Intranet zone (Tools > Internet Options > Security > Local Intranet > Sites > Advanced). 3) Configure proxy exceptions for AD FS endpoint. 4) Use browser supporting Integrated Windows Auth. 5) Verify AD connectivity: nltest /dsgetdc:<FQDN>

symptom: Federated users must sign in twice (enter username and password two times) before being prompted for MFA in AD FS with Azure MFA Server or third-party MFA provider
rootCause: MsolDomainFederationSettings has SupportsMFA=$true and PromptLoginBehavior=TranslateToFreshPasswordAuth, causing Entra ID to send wfresh=0 to ADFS which forces a fresh password login before MFA
solution: Change PromptLoginBehavior to NativeSupport: Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings -DomainName <domain> -PreferredAuthenticationProtocol WsFed -SupportsMfa $True -PromptLoginBehavior NativeSupport. Requires AD FS on Windows Server 2016 or 2012 R2 with July 2016 update KB3172614

symptom: Error setting up second federated domain: 'The federation service identifier specified in the AD FS 2.0 server is already in use' when running New-MSOLFederatedDomain or Convert-MSOLDomainToFederated
rootCause: Entra ID requires a unique federation brand URI per federated domain, but AD FS uses a global value by default for all federated trusts - second domain reuses existing URI
solution: 1) Install Update Rollup 1 for AD FS 2.0. 2) Delete existing Microsoft Office 365 Identity Platform relying party trust. 3) Re-create with -supportmultipledomain switch: Update-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName <domain> -supportmultipledomain. 4) Add new domain: New-MSOLFederatedDomain -domainname <domain> -supportmultipledomain

symptom: 使用 Microsoft Graph API 管理用户时收到 Authorization_RequestDenied: Insufficient privileges to complete the operation 错误
rootCause: App Registration 缺少所需的 API 权限或 Entra RBAC 角色；例如 disable user 需要 User.EnableDisableAccount.All + User.Read.All 且需 Grant admin consent
solution: 1) 为 App Registration 分配正确的 Entra RBAC 角色（如 User Administrator）；2) 在 API permissions 中添加所需权限；3) Grant admin consent for default directory

symptom: Android 设备上使用 ADAL 进行联合身份验证失败，AuthenticationException 错误；Chrome 中 STS 登录页被标记为不安全
rootCause: Android 不支持从证书的 authorityInformationAccess 字段下载额外证书；若 AD FS 的 SSL 证书链中间证书标记为 Extra download 而非 Sent by server，Android 无法获取完整证书链
solution: 配置 STS 和 WAP 服务器发送完整中间证书链：导出 SSL 证书时选择 PKCS#12 并勾选 Include all certificates in the certificate path，导入到服务器的 Personal certificate store

symptom: 使用 Microsoft Graph 创建对象（user/group/app）后立即 GET/PATCH/UPDATE 该对象，收到 404 Not Found 错误
rootCause: Entra ID 架构跨地理数据中心复制，新创建的对象需要数分钟完成同步，请求可能路由到尚未同步的副本
solution: 创建对象后等待一段时间再操作；若仍 404 则倍增等待时间重试（exponential backoff）

symptom: Authentication fails with error: The requested federation realm object does not exist - affects users in federated domains with third-party IdP
rootCause: Third-party identity provider returns wrong IssuerURI in the SAML response Issuer field, which does not match the federated domain configuration in Microsoft Entra ID
solution: Resolution 1: Contact third-party IdP support to correct the IssuerURI. Resolution 2: Use Set-MsolDomainFederationSettings to update the IssuerUri to match the realm object in the error. For SAMLP protocol, include -PreferredAuthenticationProtocol samlp parameter

symptom: Cannot set up MFA - error: You cannot have more than 5 hardware tokens or authenticator apps. You have too many devices registered
rootCause: Maximum of 5 device tokens already persisted in StrongAuthenticationPhoneAppDetail property of the directory user object
solution: Solution 1: Delete sign-in methods at https://aka.ms/mysecurityinfo (admin may need to set Require re-register MFA first). Solution 2: Use Graph API - GET /beta/users/{upn}/authentication/microsoftAuthenticatorMethods to list, then DELETE each authenticator by ID

symptom: Connect-MSOLService cmdlet fails with errors: MicrosoftOnlineException / Access Denied / The user name or password is incorrect - cannot connect to Office 365/Azure/Intune via PowerShell
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: module version issues, permission problems, or credential errors. MSOnline module deprecated since March 2024
solution: Refer to specific KB articles per error type: KB2887306 for Exception, KB2887685 for Access Denied, KB2887705 for username/password error. Recommended: migrate to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK

symptom: Admin cannot sign in to cloud services (Office 365/Azure/Intune) with MFA enabled after losing phone or phone number change - no text/voice call received
rootCause: MFA verification method (phone) is no longer accessible due to device loss or number change, and no alternative verification method was configured
solution: Another admin must reset MFA settings: sign in as admin -> MFA management page -> select affected user -> Manage user settings -> check Require selected users to provide contact methods again -> Save

symptom: Enterprise State Roaming settings do not sync between Windows 10 devices. Event ID 6065 logged with error 80070533: This user cannot sign in because this account is currently disabled
rootCause: Tenant not provisioned with RMSBASIC subscription needed for ESR data encryption. AllowAdHocSubscriptions set to False prevents automatic RMSBASIC provisioning
solution: Verify RMSBASIC via Get-MsolAccountSku. If missing: set AllowAdHocSubscriptions to True, disable/re-enable ESR in Entra admin center to trigger RMSBASIC provisioning. Can set AllowAdHocSubscriptions back to False after provisioning

symptom: User displayed as disabled for MFA but behaves as enabled, or vice versa. MFA status appears opposite of actual behavior
rootCause: User is a guest user added from another directory. MFA behavior is always based on home directory status, not guest directory display
solution: By design. MFA behavior follows home directory settings. To change MFA behavior, make changes in user home directory. Check if user is guest by looking for dimmed checkbox on Manage Multi-Factor Auth screen

symptom: MFA 启用的全局管理员未收到验证码短信或语音呼叫，无法登录 Office 365/Azure/Intune
rootCause: 用户账户可能被阻止使用 MFA，或手机号码配置错误
solution: 1. 选择其他验证选项重试; 2. 请另一全局管理员确认手机号码正确; 3. 检查用户是否被 Block sign in，如是则在 Azure Portal → Entra ID → Users → 该用户配置文件中禁用阻止

symptom: 用户重置密码时收到错误 "We could not verify your account"，无法完成自助密码重置
rootCause: 用户帐户没有登记电话号码用于验证
solution: 联系管理员更新用户的电话号码

symptom: 联合用户频繁被要求重新登录 Entra ID，会话仅持续 12 小时后即需重新认证
rootCause: LastPasswordChangeTimestamp 属性未同步到 Entra ID，导致会话 cookie 和 refresh token 的 Max Age 仅 12 小时
solution: 确保通过 Entra Connect 同步 PwdLastSet 属性（检查 attribute filtering 和 sync rules），或使用 PowerShell Set-MsolUser -StsRefreshTokensValidFrom 手动设置

symptom: 自助密码重置时收到错误 "We did not receive your response in time, or you hung up the call"
rootCause: 用户按错键（未按 # 键）、提前挂断电话、未接听来电、或登记的电话号码不正确
solution: 接听验证电话后按 # 键确认；如电话号码错误，联系管理员更新

symptom: 自助密码重置时收到错误 "Your request could not be processed"，重试仍然失败
rootCause: 登记的电话号码不正确，系统无法联系到用户进行验证
solution: 联系管理员更新电话号码；如可用，尝试其他验证方式（手机/办公电话/移动应用）

symptom: 运行 Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard 时报错 "The user name or password is incorrect"，Event ID 611 记录密码同步失败
rootCause: 多域 AD 森林中存在同名企业管理员账户（sAMAccountName 相同但密码不同），向导对所有域使用同一密码导致认证失败
solution: 创建 sAMAccountName 在森林中唯一的企业管理员账户；或统一所有同名账户的密码

symptom: 自助密码重置时收到错误 "Oops! We encountered an unexpected error while contacting you"
rootCause: 登记的电话号码不正确，系统无法联系到用户
solution: 联系管理员更新电话号码；尝试其他验证方式（手机/办公电话/移动应用）

symptom: Microsoft Graph delta query returns error: "Change enumeration is not supported for requested tenant" (Request_UnsupportedQuery)
rootCause: Tenant is an Azure AD B2C tenant; delta/differential queries are not supported in B2C tenants
solution: Run delta queries against a regular Microsoft Entra tenant instead of a B2C tenant

symptom: Microsoft Graph API request to get B2B guest user by UPN fails with Resource not found (Request_ResourceNotFound) when UPN contains # character
rootCause: The # character in B2B UPN (e.g. user_gmail.com#EXT#@tenant.onmicrosoft.com) is treated as URL fragment and not sent to server
solution: URL-encode the # character as %23 in the UPN (e.g. user_gmail.com%23EXT%23@tenant.onmicrosoft.com)

symptom: Microsoft Graph List users API (GET /v1.0/users) returns only one user instead of all directory users
rootCause: Signed in with a personal Microsoft account (MSA) which has no access to organizational directory data in Entra ID
solution: Sign in with an organizational Microsoft Entra account (e.g. user@tenant.onmicrosoft.com) instead of a personal MSA

symptom: Microsoft Graph memberOf API returns null values for all group/role properties except @odata.type and id
rootCause: Application lacks sufficient permissions to read the returned directory object types; without proper permissions, only type and ID are returned
solution: Grant at least Directory.Read.All permission to the application to get complete group/role property information

symptom: 403 error when modifying user mobilePhone, businessPhones, or otherMails attributes via Microsoft Graph, even with User.ReadWrite.All or Directory.ReadWrite.All permissions
rootCause: User.ReadWrite.All and Directory.ReadWrite.All permissions are insufficient to modify mobilePhone, businessPhones, and otherMails attributes. These three attributes require an admin role.
solution: Assign Helpdesk Administrator, User Administrator, or Global Administrator role to the service principal or user attempting to modify these attributes.

symptom: Error 'The identity of the calling application could not be established' when using Microsoft Graph
rootCause: The oid and sub claims are missing from the access token because the service principal doesn't exist in the tenant or the tenant isn't aware of the application.
solution: Add the service principal to the tenant by building an admin consent URL (https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/adminconsent?client_id={client-id}) and signing in with a Global Administrator account.

symptom: 401 HTTP response when looking up users via Microsoft Graph /users endpoint, despite having User.Read.All permission consented
rootCause: The authorizationPolicy object has allowedToReadOtherUsers set to false, which prevents default user roles from reading other users even with proper Graph permissions.
solution: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/policies/authorizationPolicy with {"defaultUserRolePermissions":{"allowedToReadOtherUsers":true}} to re-enable user lookup.

symptom: NoPermissionsInAccessToken error when calling Microsoft Graph /me endpoint from an application using client credentials grant flow
rootCause: The /me endpoint requires user context (delegated permissions with signed-in user). Client credentials grant flow produces application-only tokens with roles claim instead of scp claim, so /me cannot determine which user is associated.
solution: Use the /users/{upn-or-userID} endpoint instead of /me when using client credentials grant flow. Ensure the application has User.Read.All application permission.

symptom: HTTP 403 'Auth token does not contain valid permissions or user does not have valid roles' when calling Microsoft Graph Security API endpoints (/security/alert, /security/secureScores)
rootCause: Access token lacks required SecurityEvents.Read.All or SecurityEvents.ReadWrite.All permissions, or the authenticating user doesn't have Security Reader/Security Administrator/Global Administrator role (required for delegated tokens).
solution: 1) Add SecurityEvents.Read.All (GET) or SecurityEvents.ReadWrite.All (GET/POST/PATCH) permissions to the app. 2) For delegated tokens, ensure user has Security Reader, Security Administrator, or Global Administrator role. Check wids claim in token. 3) Avoid implicit grant flow which may omit wids claim.

symptom: Error 'Authentication_RequestFromNonPremiumTenantOrB2CTenant: Neither tenant is B2C or tenant doesn't have premium license' when querying sign-in activities or user registration details via Graph API
rootCause: The tenant lacks Entra ID Premium P1/P2 license required for auditLogs/signIns, user signInActivity, and UserRegistrationDetails endpoints. Also, using only AuditLog.Read.All without Directory.Read.All can cause intermittent failures.
solution: 1) Ensure tenant has Entra ID Premium P1 or P2 license. 2) For sign-in activities: grant both AuditLog.Read.All AND Directory.Read.All. 3) For registration details: grant Reports.Read.All and assign Reports Reader/Security Reader/Security Administrator/Global Reader/Global Administrator role.

symptom: Microsoft Graph PowerShell Connect-MgGraph -AccessToken throws 'IDX14102: Unable to decode the header'
rootCause: Az.Accounts module version 5.0+ returns tokens as SecureString by default. Connect-MgGraph (v2.28.0+) expects a plain string, causing decode failure when SecureString is passed directly.
solution: Convert the SecureString token to plain text: $token = Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl 'https://graph.microsoft.com'; Connect-MgGraph -AccessToken (ConvertFrom-SecureString -SecureString $token.Token -AsPlainText). Alternatively, downgrade Az.Accounts to v4.2.0.

symptom: Python scripts making Microsoft Graph requests get 403 Forbidden with 'Microsoft-Azure-Application-Gateway/v2' error, detected as web crawlers
rootCause: Python scripts using pool manager don't structure HTTP requests to conform to expected patterns, causing the gateway to misidentify them as web crawlers
solution: Use Microsoft Graph SDK for Python, or use Python's Session object (requests.Session) to send requests. Set unique User-Agent header and include Accept: application/json header

symptom: Password writeback fails with error code 8023062C when changing or resetting password in Entra ID; message says server not configured for insecure setting or 128-bit TLS/SSL required
rootCause: Microsoft Entra Connect is configured without requiring LDAP traffic signing and encryption
solution: Enable 'Sign and Encrypt LDAP Traffic' in Synchronization Service Manager in three places: AD Forest connection options, Directory Partition connection options, and Alternate Credentials options. Restart Azure AD Sync service afterwards

symptom: Synchronized Entra admin gets SSPR_009 error: 'password reset isn't turned on for your organization' when trying self-service password reset
rootCause: SSPR for Administrators (SSPR-A) is not enabled on the tenant. SSPR-A is separate from SSPR for Users (SSPR-U), and Azure portal SSPR management only controls SSPR-U
solution: Enable SSPR-A via PowerShell: Import-Module Microsoft.Graph.Identity.SignIns; Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -BodyParameter @{allowedToUseSSPR = $true}

symptom: SSPR_0029 error: 'Your organization hasn't properly set up the on-premises configuration for password reset' for synced AD admin in protected group
rootCause: User belongs to on-premises AD protected group (Domain Admins etc). Password writeback does not support resetting passwords for accounts in AD protected groups — by design
solution: No fix — this is by design. Admins in AD protected groups can change passwords in the cloud but cannot reset a forgotten password via SSPR + writeback

symptom: SSPR_0029 error during password reset; Exchange Online remote PowerShell not enabled for admin performing reset
rootCause: Exchange Online remote PowerShell disabled for the admin account performing password writeback
solution: Enable Exchange Online remote PowerShell for the admin: Set-User -Identity admin -RemotePowerShellEnabled true

symptom: Admin resets user password in Microsoft 365 admin center but user can't sign in to on-premises AD with the new password; old password still works
rootCause: Microsoft 365 admin center does not use SSPR/password writeback libraries. Password is reset only in Entra ID, not written back to on-premises AD, causing password out-of-sync
solution: Use Azure portal (portal.azure.com) to reset passwords instead of Microsoft 365 admin center (admin.microsoft.com), so password writeback properly syncs the new password to on-premises AD

symptom: After forced password change on first sign-in, user sees 'Your password was successfully updated, but our servers take a little time to catch up' warning
rootCause: Timing issue with Pass-through Authentication (PTA): user signs in before password hash sync finishes syncing temporary password to Entra ID, causing password mismatch during writeback verification
solution: Wait at least 2 minutes after on-premises password reset before signing in to allow password hash synchronization to sync the temporary password. This is expected behavior with PTA + forced password change

symptom: Admin resets user password via Azure portal but receives error: Unfortunately, you cannot reset this user password because your on-premises policy does not allow it
rootCause: Password writeback works but new password does not meet on-premises AD password policy (complexity, length, history, or minimum age)
solution: Check DC Event Viewer Security log for Event ID 4724 Audit Failure. Review on-premises AD password policy. Ensure new password meets all requirements.

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard returns: An unknown error occurred with the Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant
rootCause: Microsoft Online Services Sign-In Assistant service is corrupted or not started
solution: Uninstall Sign-In Assistant and Azure AD Sync tool via appwiz.cpl, reinstall. If persists, verify Sign-In Assistant service is Started in services.msc.

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard: The Enterprise Administrator credentials that you supplied are not valid
rootCause: Account lacks Enterprise Administrator permissions in on-premises AD forest or wrong credentials
solution: Verify credentials. Open dsa.msc, find Enterprise Admins group, check Members. Add account to Enterprise Admins or use a member credential.

symptom: Microsoft Entra Connect ADSync service fails to start
rootCause: ADSync service account lacks User Rights Assignment (Allow log on locally, Log on as batch job, Log on as service) due to domain GPO override, or account expired/disabled
solution: Check gpedit.msc User Rights Assignment. Run gpresult /H to check domain GPO. Update GPO or add ADSync account. Verify service account status.

symptom: Entra Management Agent hangs during Full/Delta Import with KeyNotFoundException (Event 6801/109)
rootCause: Device object type not selected in Entra Connector properties
solution: FIM Sync console > Connectors > Entra ID > Properties > Select Object Types > enable device checkbox > re-run import.

symptom: Entra Connect automatic upgrade partial: scheduler suspended or password sync/writeback disabled post-upgrade
rootCause: Bug in automatic upgrade for Entra Connect 1.1.443.0 and earlier causes Upgrader.exe crash with unhandled exception
solution: Check AADConnect AutoUpgrader logs. Run Entra Connect wizard > Upgrade. Verify Get-ADSyncScheduler SchedulerSuspended=False. Review solution for password sync/writeback.

symptom: Connect-AipService fails: The attempt to connect to Azure Information Protection service failed, or Object reference not set to an instance of an object
rootCause: Wrong credentials, not global admin, no AIP subscription, network blocking *.aadrm.com:443, or using PowerShell 7 (incompatible)
solution: Verify global admin creds. Confirm AIP subscription. Allow *.aadrm.com and *.cloudapp.net port 443. Use PowerShell 5 not 7.

symptom: Cannot enable Device writeback option in Entra Connect wizard
rootCause: Entra org not enabled for device writeback, or FSMO role holders not replicating correctly
solution: Run repadmin /showrepl to check AD replication. netdom query FSMO and seize if needed. Run Initialize-ADSyncDeviceWriteBack from AdPrep folder. Re-open wizard.

symptom: Running DirSyncConfigShell.psc1 on Windows Server 2008 R2 fails with 'Could not load file or assembly' error after installing Directory Sync tool v6765.0006
rootCause: Windows Management Framework 3.0 is not installed on the Windows Server 2008 R2 machine; the assembly requires a newer runtime
solution: Install Windows Management Framework 3.0 on the Server 2008 R2 machine, or run the Directory Sync tool from a Windows Server 2012 R2 machine

symptom: Security group remains mail-enabled in Entra ID after removing email address from on-premises AD and running sync; group keeps original onmicrosoft.com address as primary
rootCause: When a mail-enabled security group syncs to Entra ID, an onmicrosoft.com secondary email is appended. Deleting the primary email promotes the secondary, so the group stays mail-enabled
solution: Delete the security group from Admin Portal then re-sync, or move the group to an unsynced OU to remove it from cloud, then move it back

symptom: Entra ID joined computer experiences approximately 3-hour delay (black screen with spinning dots) during boot when using federated users
rootCause: When the workgroup name matches the on-premises AD domain NetBIOS name, Windows evaluates a condition that triggers a 10,000-second (≈167 min) timeout
solution: Change the workgroup name so it does not match the NetBIOS name of the on-premises domain

symptom: Directory synchronization to Entra ID stops working or admin receives warning that sync hasn't registered in more than 24 hours; may see Stopped-Server-Down or Stopped-Extension-DLL-Exception in MIISClient
rootCause: The admin account or service logon account used for directory sync was deleted, disabled, or its password expired; or network issues or stopped sync services
solution: Verify sync service is started, check admin account can sign in, reset password if needed, re-run configuration wizard; for service account issues check AD account status and reset password on the service logon properties

symptom: SMTP proxy addresses not synced to Entra ID; portal shows 'duplicate Proxy address conflict' or email report says attributes modified due to association with another object
rootCause: Another object in the directory has the same SMTP proxy address, causing a duplicate conflict during sync
solution: Use IdFix DirSync Error Remediation Tool to identify duplicate attributes in on-premises AD; check Entra ID via portal (Users with errors view) or PowerShell; change conflicting email addresses to be unique

symptom: Password Hash Synchronization is automatically enabled when configuring Pass-through Authentication via Change user sign-in task in Entra Connect versions 1.1.557.0-1.1.614.0
rootCause: Bug in Entra Connect versions 1.1.557.0 through 1.1.614.0 where the Change user sign-in task still enables Password Sync when enabling PTA, though this prerequisite was removed
solution: Upgrade to Entra Connect version 1.1.644.0 or later; to disable, run Entra Connect → Configure → Customize synchronization options → clear Password synchronization checkbox

symptom: Entra Connect upgrade fails with error code 1603 Unable to install the synchronization service at DetectServiceAccount action
rootCause: Service account configured using UPN format instead of DomainSamAccountName
solution: Open Services > Microsoft Azure AD Sync > Properties > Log On tab > set account using DomainSamAccountName format instead of UPN

symptom: Table-valued parameter (TVP) errors, password hash sync failures, or staging-error discovery errors after installing Entra Connect on Windows Server 2019; Event ID 6301 with 0x80004005 in rcvtvp.h
rootCause: The 'Beta: Use Unicode UTF-8 for worldwide language support' setting is enabled in Windows Region settings; the LocalDB database version in Server 2019 does not support this format
solution: Open Control Panel → Region → Administrative → Change System locale → clear 'Use Unicode UTF-8 for worldwide language support' checkbox → restart server

symptom: Running Azure AD Sync tool configuration wizard fails with error: Failed to get address for method: CreateIdentityHandle2 from library msoidcli.dll. GetLastError code: 127
rootCause: Earlier version of the Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant is installed on the directory sync computer
solution: Uninstall all versions of Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant, then install the latest version from the official Microsoft download link

symptom: Cannot install Azure Active Directory module for Windows PowerShell; error: You must have Windows PowerShell 2.0 or greater installed on this computer
rootCause: PowerShell 2.0 is not enabled or user is not logged on as local admin
solution: Log on as local admin (not just domain admin), ensure Windows PowerShell 2.0 feature is enabled in Windows Features, then install the Azure AD module

symptom: After changing domain-based filtering in AD management agent/connector, sync fails with 'missing-partition-for-run-step' error and Event ID 6020 is logged
rootCause: Run profile is not updated after domain-based filtering change; partition configuration references a removed or missing partition
solution: For removed domain: delete the step with alphanumeric Partition value in each run profile, then run Full Sync. For added domain: add a new step for the new partition in each run profile, then run Full Sync

symptom: High CPU usage (up to 100%) on Microsoft Entra Connect Health for Sync server; Microsoft.Online.Reporting.MonitoringAgent.Startup process consuming CPU
rootCause: June 2018 update for .NET Framework 4.7.2 causes incompatibility with the Connect Health for Sync monitoring agent
solution: For AD DS/AD FS: install Connect Health agent version 3.1.7.0+. For Entra Connect: install the latest version of Microsoft Entra Connect

symptom: After entering enterprise admin credentials in Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard, error: LogonUser() Failed with error code: 1789
rootCause: Computer running Directory Sync tool cannot authenticate with Active Directory
solution: Restart the computer, or unjoin from domain (join workgroup) then rejoin the domain and restart

symptom: Installing Azure Active Directory Sync Tool fails with error: The computer must be joined to a domain
rootCause: Computer is not joined to an AD DS domain, or the computer does not register a host (A) resource record with DNS
solution: Join the computer to an AD DS domain. If already joined, fix DNS settings: ensure correct DNS server configuration, run ipconfig /flushdns and ipconfig /registerdns, restart if needed

symptom: Identity sync client (Entra Connect / Azure AD Sync) cannot authenticate to Entra ID; error Unable to communicate with the Windows Azure Active Directory service with Error 906 or Event ID 0; wizard shows Failed even after 5 retries Action: PingProvisioningServiceEndPoint
rootCause: .NET Framework does not recognize the proxy settings; the sync appliance tries to make a direct connection to the Internet instead of using the unauthenticated proxy server
solution: Edit C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\Config\machine.config and add <system.net><defaultProxy><proxy usesystemdefault="true" proxyaddress="http://<PROXYIP>:80" bypassonlocal="true" /></defaultProxy></system.net> with the actual proxy IP

symptom: ADSync service fails to start on Entra Connect 2.x with SQL LocalDB; Event ID 528 from SQLLocalDB 15.0; Event IDs 2005/6226 from ADSync with 0x8023044a; error.log shows Error 9003 log scan number passed to log scan in database model is not valid
rootCause: Bug in SQL Server 2019 LocalDB backup logic creates inconsistent state in Model database; sets dbi_status to FULL recovery mode while Master database governs SIMPLE mode, causing log truncation mismatch after restart
solution: Mitigation: navigate to ADSync2019 instance folder, rename model.mdf/modellog.ldf to old_*, copy fresh template files from C:\Program Files\Microsoft SQL Server\150\LocalDB\Binn\Templates, restart ADSync service. Permanent fix: upgrade to Entra Connect version 2.1.1.0 or later

symptom: Directory sync error: This company has exceeded the number of objects that can be synchronized (Error 016)
rootCause: Objects exceed Entra ID directory quota (50k without verified domain, 300k with). Deleted objects in tombstone (30 days) also count at 25%
solution: Add verified domain to increase quota to 300k; or contact Microsoft Support for quota increase beyond 300k

symptom: User primary group membership not synced by Entra Connect
rootCause: Entra Connect does not support PrimaryGroupID attribute
solution: Known limitation. Avoid primary group for groups needing sync, use regular membership

symptom: Password hash synchronization stops working after updating global admin credentials in FIM; Event IDs 115, 0, 655, 6900 logged with Access denied or user name or password is incorrect
rootCause: Updated global administrator credentials in FIM cause password sync authentication failures
solution: Run the Azure Active Directory Sync tool Configuration Wizard; alternatively restart the Forefront Identity Manager Synchronization Service

symptom: Password is not synced from Entra ID to on-premises directory after password change or reset; user sees Your request could not be processed error during SSPR
rootCause: Multiple causes: password writeback prerequisites not met, permissions not configured, password reset agent not running, or network connectivity issue between Entra ID and local Entra Connect
solution: Verify password writeback prerequisites; check permissions; ensure password reset agent is running; verify network connectivity. Note: M365 admin center and PowerShell V1/V2 password resets do NOT support writeback

symptom: Password hash synchronization stops working; Event ID 611 logged: Password synchronization failed for domain
rootCause: Multiple known issues: RPC Error 8439 (Win2003 DC), RPC Error 8593 (domain rename), ArgumentOutOfRangeException, duplicate key, or RPC Error 8453 (AD DS Connector Account missing Replicating Directory Changes permissions)
solution: Update to latest Entra Connect version; for RPC 8453 grant Replicating Directory Changes and Replicating Directory Changes All extended permissions to AD DS Connector Account

symptom: Password not syncing from Entra ID to on-prem after change/reset; user sees request could not be processed
rootCause: Writeback prerequisites not met, permissions wrong, reset agent not running, or network issue
solution: Verify writeback prerequisites/permissions; ensure reset agent running; check connectivity; note M365 admin center resets do NOT support writeback

symptom: PHS stops after several days; Event ID 611 Password synchronization failed for domain
rootCause: RPC 8439 (Server 2003 DCs), RPC 8593 (domain rename), invalid FileTime, duplicate key, or RPC 8453 (missing replication permissions)
solution: Update to latest Entra Connect; for RPC 8453 grant Replicating Directory Changes + All permissions to AD DS Connector Account

symptom: Permission-issue export error 8344 Insufficient access rights on AD connector in Sync Service Manager Export
rootCause: MSOL_ connector account lacks permissions to write back object properties; AdminCount or SDProp issues can block propagation
solution: Use Connect wizard Troubleshooting > Configure AD DS Connector Account Permissions; or ADSyncConfig PowerShell; or dsacls

symptom: Users must use different passwords for computer logon and cloud service (Office 365/Azure/Intune) after password sync is enabled with Azure AD Sync tool
rootCause: Cloud service password was changed independently; password synchronization only syncs from on-premises AD to cloud, not the reverse
solution: Have user change their on-premises computer password (or admin resets it with 'User must change password at next logon' unchecked); after directory sync, passwords will match

symptom: Delta Import from on-premises AD connector fails in Synchronization Service Manager; Connection Log shows dropped-connection status with 'Size Limit Exceeded' error code 0x4; Event ID 6050 logged
rootCause: LDAP search hit the default 1000-record MaxPageSize limit, typically after restoring a large number of objects from AD Recycle Bin simultaneously
solution: Option 1: Run a Full Import on the AD DS connector instead of Delta Import. Option 2: Temporarily increase MaxPageSize via ntdsutil (e.g. to 5000), run sync, then restore to 1000

symptom: High CPU usage and slow performance from Microsoft.Online.Reporting.MonitoringAgent.Startup process on Entra Connect server
rootCause: Connect Health for Sync monitoring agent incompatible with .NET Framework 4.7.2 or July 2018 .NET updates
solution: Update Connect Health Agent to v3.1.7.0+ (for AD DS/AD FS); or install latest Entra Connect version (for sync); auto-upgrade will also resolve

symptom: New-MsolDomain cmdlet fails: 'Unable to add this domain. It is a subdomain and its authentication type is different from the authentication type of the root domain' when adding subdomain to federated domain
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain adds subdomains as standard authentication, conflicting with parent domain's federated authentication type
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain> instead of New-MSOLDomain to add subdomains under federated root domains

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails with 'Unable to authenticate your credentials. Make sure that your user name is in the format: <username>@<domain>' error
rootCause: Either incorrect username format (not UPN format) or user account is enabled for Microsoft Entra MFA which is not supported by MSOnline PowerShell module
solution: 1) Use UPN format user@domain.com (not 'user' or 'domain\user'). 2) If MFA enabled: disable MFA for the account or use a different admin account without MFA. Consider migrating to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK which supports MFA

symptom: Microsoft Entra Connect wizard shows 'Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license' during password writeback setup
rootCause: Three possible causes: 1) Admin account lacks appropriate Entra subscription license, 2) Server time is out of sync with Microsoft cloud services, 3) TLS 1.2 not enabled
solution: 1) Enable TLS 1.2 on the server. 2) Ensure admin account is a cloud admin (not federated) with proper license. 3) Sync server time with authoritative time server. Check AADConnect logs in %appdata%\Local\AADConnect for service bus connection errors

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails with 'Error Unknown error (0x80005000)'
rootCause: The sync tool cannot configure the domain because one or more domain controllers are unreachable or not running
solution: Run PowerShell: $Forest = [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains; $Forest — examine output for domains with missing information (empty DomainControllers, Forest, DomainMode fields), then investigate and fix the unreachable domain controller

symptom: User object not synced to Entra ID — only AD connector shows in metaverse, no Entra connector listed, no error returned. msExchRecipientTypeDetails value is 2
rootCause: When msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox), the sync rule sets sourceAnchor to NULL, causing AADSync to wait for the master account from account forest to join before provisioning. Without account-resource forest topology this blocks sync indefinitely
solution: 1) Ensure the master user account in Account forest is synchronized first (for account-resource forest topology), OR 2) Change msExchRecipientTypeDetails from 2 to 1 (or any non-filtered value) if not actually a linked mailbox scenario

symptom: Users in a federated domain see a yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 applications
rootCause: Office 2013 uses IDCRL (Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant) which detects federated domain and tries to authenticate to Entra ID. If user is not synced to Entra ID, authentication fails silently showing yellow exclamation mark
solution: Option 1: Set registry key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\15.0\Common\SignIn\SignInOptions to 3 (forces local AD auth only) — only for non-synced users. Option 2: Upgrade to Office 2016+ which does not exhibit this behavior

symptom: New-MsolDomain command fails: Unable to add this domain. It is a subdomain and its authentication type is different from the root domain
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain tries to add subdomain as managed auth, but parent root domain is federated - auth type mismatch
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain instead: Connect to Entra ID via PowerShell, run New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain>

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails: Unable to authenticate your credentials. Make sure user name is in format <username>@<domain>
rootCause: Incorrect username format (john or contosojohn instead of john@contoso.com), or account enabled for Entra MFA which legacy Azure AD PowerShell does not support
solution: Use correct UPN format user@domain.com. If MFA: disable MFA for admin, use non-MFA admin, or migrate to Graph PowerShell SDK with modern auth

symptom: Entra Connect wizard error: Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license
rootCause: Admin account lacks Entra ID P1/P2 license, server time out of sync, or TLS 1.2 not enabled
solution: Enable TLS 1.2; use cloud-only admin with proper license (not federated); sync server time. Check logs at %appdata%LocalAADConnect

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails: Unknown error (0x80005000)
rootCause: Sync Tool cannot reach one or more domains in the forest - child domain controller offline or unreachable
solution: Run [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains to list domains; identify ones with empty fields; bring unreachable DC online

symptom: User object missing from Entra connector in Azure AD Sync - only AD connector on Connectors tab, no error; msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2
rootCause: msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox) causes sourceAnchor rule to set NULL; AADSync waits for master account from account forest
solution: If not account-resource topology: change msExchRecipientTypeDetails from 2 to 1 in on-prem AD. If account-resource: ensure master account syncs first

symptom: Users in federated domain see yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 apps when not synced to Entra ID
rootCause: Office 2013 IDCRL detects federated domain, tries Entra ID auth, but user not synced so does not exist there
solution: Set registry HKCUSoftwareMicrosoftOffice.0CommonSignInSignInOptions=3 (local AD only, non-synced users). Or upgrade to Office 2016+

symptom: New-MsolDomain fails adding subdomain to federated domain: Unable to add this domain. Authentication type differs from root domain
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain tries to add subdomain as standard auth but parent is federated
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain> instead

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails with Unable to authenticate your credentials error (0x80048862)
rootCause: Incorrect username format (must be user@domain.com) or MFA enabled on account which MSOnline module does not support
solution: Use correct UPN format; if MFA enabled, disable MFA or use non-MFA admin account or migrate to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK

symptom: Entra Connect wizard: Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license
rootCause: Admin account lacks Entra ID license, server time out of sync, or TLS 1.2 not enabled
solution: Enable TLS 1.2; use cloud admin account with proper license; sync server time with authoritative time server

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails with Unknown error (0x80005000)
rootCause: Config Wizard cannot reach one or more domains in the forest; domain controller is down or unreachable
solution: Run [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains in PowerShell to find unreachable domain; restore DC connectivity

symptom: User object not synced to Entra ID; Entra connector missing in metaverse; msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox)
rootCause: msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 makes Entra Connect wait for master account from account forest before provisioning; without account-resource topology the object appears filtered
solution: Change msExchRecipientTypeDetails from 2 to 1 in on-premises AD if not using account-resource forest topology; or ensure master account syncs first

symptom: Users in federated domain not synced to Entra ID see yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 apps
rootCause: Office 2013 IDCRL detects federated domain and tries Entra ID auth; user not synced so auth fails showing warning
solution: Set registry HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\15.0\Common\SignIn\SignInOptions to 3 (local AD auth only); or upgrade to Office 2016+. Do NOT set for synced users

symptom: Some users cannot sign in to M365/Entra/Intune; password appears not synced. 'User must change password at next logon' is checked on the AD account.
rootCause: Password hash sync does not sync temporary passwords when 'User must change password at next logon' is selected, unless ForcePasswordChangeOnLogOn feature is enabled.
solution: Clear the 'User must change password at next logon' checkbox, have the user change their password, or enable the ForcePasswordChangeOnLogOn feature in Entra Connect.

symptom: Users can sign in with old password but not new password after directory sync or password sync was disabled and re-enabled.
rootCause: Disabling and re-enabling directory/password sync may not trigger an immediate full password sync; old password hashes remain valid until new sync completes.
solution: Re-enable password sync via Entra Connect wizard (Customize synchronization options), then run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Initial to force a full password sync.

symptom: Event ID 611 logged: Password synchronization failed for domain with RPC Error 8439 (distinguished name invalid) or RPC Error 8593 (different replication epochs).
rootCause: Windows Server 2003 domain controllers handle certain replication scenarios unexpectedly; or a domain rename is in progress causing epoch mismatch.
solution: For RPC 8439: investigate Windows Server 2003 DC behavior. For RPC 8593/ArgumentOutOfRange/duplicate key errors: update to latest version of Entra Connect.

symptom: Event ID 652/655 logged: 'Password Synchronization has not been activated for this company' during credential provisioning or ping.
rootCause: Password sync feature is not enabled for the tenant, or Entra credentials were updated through FIM without re-running the configuration wizard.
solution: Enable password sync via Entra Connect configuration wizard. If credentials were updated in FIM, re-run the Entra Connect wizard to refresh credentials.

symptom: New-MsolDomain fails with error: 'Unable to add this domain. It is a subdomain and its authentication type is different from the authentication type of the root domain.'
rootCause: New-MSOLDomain tries to add a subdomain as standard authentication, but the parent domain is configured for federated authentication.
solution: Use New-MSOLFederatedDomain -DomainName:<subdomain> instead of New-MSOLDomain to add subdomains under a federated parent domain.

symptom: Connect-MsolService fails with 'Unable to authenticate your credentials. Make sure that your user name is in the format: <username>@<domain>.'
rootCause: Either incorrect username format (must be user@domain, not domain\user) or the admin account has MFA enabled which is not supported by MSOnline PowerShell module.
solution: Use UPN format (user@domain.com) for username. If MFA is enabled, either disable MFA for the account or use a different admin account without MFA. Consider migrating to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK.

symptom: Entra Connect wizard shows error 'Unable to configure password writeback. Ensure you have the required license' during password writeback setup.
rootCause: Admin account lacks appropriate Entra license, server time is out of sync with Microsoft cloud services, or TLS 1.2 is not enabled.
solution: Enable TLS 1.2, ensure admin account is a cloud admin (not federated) with proper license, and verify server time is synchronized with authoritative time server.

symptom: Azure AD Sync Tool Configuration Wizard fails with 'Error Unknown error (0x80005000)'.
rootCause: The sync tool cannot configure the domain because one or more domain controllers are unreachable or unhealthy.
