solution: Check domain controller health using PowerShell: [System.DirectoryServices.ActiveDirectory.Forest]::GetCurrentForest().domains. Identify and fix any unreachable domain controllers.

symptom: User object not synced to Entra ID; only AD connector shown in metaverse (no Entra connector). msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 on the user object. No sync error reported.
rootCause: When msExchRecipientTypeDetails=2 (Linked Mailbox), Entra Connect sets sourceAnchor to NULL and waits for the master account from account forest to join. Without account-resource forest topology, the object appears stuck/filtered.
solution: If not using account-resource forest topology, change msExchRecipientTypeDetails to 1 or another non-filtered value. If using account-resource forest, ensure the master account in account forest is synchronized first.

symptom: Users in a federated domain see a yellow exclamation mark in Office 2013 applications. The user exists in federated domain but is not synced to Entra ID.
rootCause: Office 2013 uses IDCRL (Microsoft Online Services Sign-in Assistant) which detects the federated domain and tries to authenticate to Entra ID. Since the user is not synced, authentication fails silently showing the yellow mark.
solution: Set registry key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\15.0\Common\SignIn\SignInOptions=3 to force local AD authentication only. Alternatively, upgrade to Office 2016+ which does not have this issue.

symptom: Hybrid-joined device state changes from registered to pending after the device object is moved to an OU outside Entra Connect sync scope and then moved back. Device cannot complete re-registration.
rootCause: When the device object moves out of the sync scope OU, Entra Connect deletes it from Entra ID. Moving back creates a new pending object, but the device fails registration because it was previously registered with the old object.
solution: Run dsregcmd /leave at an elevated command prompt, then restart the device. It will reinitiate registration via the Automatic-Device-Join scheduled task under Task Scheduler > Microsoft > Windows > Workplace Join.

symptom: Users blocked from signing in to Microsoft Entra ID after IPv6 is enabled on the network. Conditional Access policies or per-user MFA trusted IPs do not include IPv6 addresses, causing sign-in requests from IPv6 egress points to be denied.
rootCause: Microsoft Entra ID service endpoints resolve to both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. If the organization has public IPv6 addresses not added to named locations or Conditional Access policies, sign-in attempts over IPv6 are treated as coming from unknown/untrusted locations and get blocked by location-based CA policies.
solution: 1. Identify public-facing IPv6 addresses (partner with network admin/ISP). 2. Add IPv6 ranges to named locations in Entra ID (Conditional Access > Named locations). 3. Update relevant Conditional Access policies to include the new named locations. 4. For per-user MFA: add IPv6 addresses to trusted IPs. 5. Test with NRPT rule (Resolve-DnsName login.microsoftonline.com) to verify IPv6 connectivity before production rollout. 6. Use Sign-in logs filtered by IP containing ":" to find IPv6 sign-in attempts.

symptom: ASP.NET or ASP.NET Core web application redirects users to re-authenticate every hour. AJAX calls may fail with CORS errors to login.microsoftonline.com when the session expires mid-use.
rootCause: By default, Microsoft Entra ID tokens (ID tokens, access tokens, SAML tokens) expire after 1 hour. ASP.NET/ASP.NET Core middleware sets authentication ticket expiration to match the token lifetime (UseTokenLifetime = true by default), causing users to be redirected for re-authentication after 1 hour.
solution: Set UseTokenLifetime = false in OpenIdConnectOptions and customize the authentication ticket expiration in OnTokenValidated event: context.Properties.ExpiresUtc = DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.AddHours(12). For ASP.NET (OWIN): set UseTokenLifetime = false in OpenIdConnectAuthenticationOptions and set ExpiresUtc in CookieAuthenticationProvider.OnResponseSignIn. For WS-Federation: use OnSecurityTokenValidated event. Note: extending ticket expiration means users may retain access even after being disabled in Entra ID until the ticket expires.
